Let $f(x)$ be a twice continuously differentiable in the interval $(0, \infty)$. If
$$ \lim_{x \to \infty} (x^2 f''(x) + 4x f'(x) + 2 f(x)) = 1, $$
find $\displaystyle{\lim_{x \to \infty} f(x)}$ and $\displaystyle{\lim_{x \to \infty} x f'(x)}$.
Do \textbf{not} assume any special form of $f(x)$. Hint: use l'Hôpital's rule.
